item.title: Elegy of Mess-Telmann
item.description: <h4 id="The-following-is-an-elegy-written-for-Mess-Telmann-around-the-7th-century-AD"><a href="#The-following-is-an-elegy-written-for-Mess-Telmann-around-the-7th-century-AD" class="headerlink" title="The following is an elegy written for Mess-Telmann around the 7th century AD."></a>The following is an elegy written for <em>Mess-Telmann</em> around the 7th century AD.</h4><table class="bilingual-table" style="color:rgb(201, 202, 204);"><thead><tr><th> Old Irish </th><th> English </th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td> Mal ad-rualaid iaṫu marb, </td><td> A prince who has reached the realms of the dead </td></tr><tr><td> macc soer Setnai, </td><td> the noble son of Setnae </td></tr><tr><td> selaig sraṫu Fomoire </td><td> laid waste the vales of the Fomoire </td></tr><tr><td> fo doine domnaib. </td><td> under the worlds of men. </td></tr><tr><td><br></td></tr><tr><td> Di oċtur Alinne </td><td> From the heights of Ailenn, </td></tr><tr><td> ort triunu talman </td><td> the powerful tribune great in dominions Mess-Telmann </td></tr><tr><td> trebun tren tuathmar, </td><td> of the Domnonian tribe [Fir Domnann] </td></tr><tr><td> Mess-Telmann Domnon. </td><td> slew the mighty of the earth. </td></tr></tbody></table>

item.title: Lebor Gabála Éirinn
